cpRatioNote: ESTIMATE ONLY. The I-485 inventory counts adjustment-of-status (in-US) cases. A small additional share of applicants are abroad in consular processing and are NOT in this inventory. We approximate the overall line by multiplying the I-485 count by this ratio. 1.03 ≈ +3%; this is a rough modeling assumption, not a USCIS figure — verify/refine.
granularityNote: USCIS reports priority-date buckets by YEAR only (with a grouped 'and earlier' floor and a most-recent listed year). We replicate that exact granularity and never invent finer (e.g. monthly) buckets. A bucket with grouped:'earlier' means 'this year and all earlier priority dates'.
todoNote: TODO: every count below is transcribed from a secondary summary of the USCIS Feb 3, 2026 report and MUST be verified cell-by-cell against the official USCIS spreadsheet at the source URL (the India/China/All Other workbooks) before launch. Mexico and the Philippines are also broken out in the official report (together ~8,500 of the 172,670 total) but are not yet seeded here.
